title: Palaquium quercifolium
taxonomy: Palaquium quercifolium is classified in the family Sapotaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the sapodilla family. This family consists of over 200 species of trees and shrubs, many of which are native to tropical regions. Palaquium quercifolium is further classified in the genus Palaquium, which is a group of sapote trees native to Southeast Asia. The species is also known to be a part of the order Ebenales. Palaquium quercifolium's classification helps in understanding its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants in the same family.

intro: Palaquium quercifolium, also known as the oak-leafed Palaquium, is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Palaquium quercifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Palaquium quercifolium is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
